rationale: Both candidates surface real, diff-grounded issues and both effectively hit the latent gap: X pinpoints five concrete pre-existing tests that will now break under the new level=2 wake guard (precisely matching the CI-break history), while Y independently flags the level=2-default-bricks-the-engine footgun and recommends loosening it, closer to the actual eventual fix. Y is broader and covers more of the human-review threads (extends the regression-test ask by noting the tests are hardware-gated and never run in normal CI, and extends the enum suggestion by pointing out it's only half-used), giving it higher recall, while X's redundant same-class findings give it marginally higher precision. Actionability is comparable, with Y's prioritized verdict slightly ahead.
